children.text: means any authorization required under Federal law with respect to an application for authorization under or a certificate of public convenience and necessity under ; andsection 717b of this titlesection 717f of this title

includes any permits, special use authorizations, certifications, opinions, or other approvals as may be required under Federal law with respect to an application for authorization under or a certificate of public convenience and necessity under .section 717b of this titlesection 717f of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Commission shall act as the lead agency for the purposes of coordinating all applicable Federal authorizations and for the purposes of complying with the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: Other agencies
children.label: 2 Other agencies
children.text: Each Federal and State agency considering an aspect of an application for Federal authorization shall cooperate with the Commission and comply with the deadlines established by the Commission.

children.label: A
children.text: ensure expeditious completion of all such proceedings; and

children.label: B
children.text: comply with applicable schedules established by Federal law.

children.heading: Failure to meet schedule
children.label: 2 Failure to meet schedule
children.text: If a Federal or State administrative agency does not complete a proceeding for an approval that is required for a Federal authorization in accordance with the schedule established by the Commission, the applicant may pursue remedies under .section 717r(d) of this title

appeals or reviews under the Coastal Zone Management Act of 1972 ( et seq.), provided that the record may be supplemented as expressly provided pursuant to section 319 of that Act []; or16 U.S.C. 145116 U.S.C. 1465

judicial review under of decisions made or actions taken of Federal and State administrative agencies and officials, provided that, if the Court determines that the record does not contain sufficient information, the Court may remand the proceeding to the Commission for further development of the consolidated record.section 717r(d) of this title

children.heading: Hearings; parties
children.label: e Hearings; parties
children.text: Hearings under this chapter may be held before the Commission, any member or members thereof, or any representative of the Commission designated by it, and appropriate records thereof shall be kept. In any proceeding before it, the Commission in accordance with such rules and regulations as it may prescribe, may admit as a party any interested State, State commission, municipality or any representative of interested consumers or security holders, or any competitor of a party to such proceeding, or any other person whose participation in the proceeding may be in the public interest.

children.heading: Procedure
children.label: f Procedure
children.text: All hearings, investigations, and proceedings under this chapter shall be governed by rules of practice and procedure to be adopted by the Commission, and in the conduct thereof the technical rules of evidence need not be applied. No informality in any hearing, investigation, or proceeding or in the manner of taking testimony shall invalidate any order, decision, rule, or regulation issued under the authority of this chapter.
